text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on, small caps led, semis went vertical. IWM gained 0.93% while The Semi 7 ripped 7.71% as AI-capex semis and Fab 7 leadership pulled indices to new highs despite SPY lagging internal breadth at +0.14% versus a +0.42% universe median.[INDICES][THE 7s][BREADTH] VIX sat at 16.3 and Fear & Greed at 61.6 as investors leaned into high-beta growth and semis while fading gold, software, and defensives in a still-neutral macro and rates backdrop.[VOL SIGNALS][FEAR & GREED][MACRO]  

INDICES — VIX closed at 16.3, below its 20-day moving average of 17.4.[VOL SIGNALS] Index performance ranked IWM +0.93%, IJH +0.91%, IJR +0.89%, DIA +0.51%, QQQ +0.46%, RSP +0.40%, SLV +0.47%, GLD +0.17%, SPY +0.14% with STYLE showing SMALL > LARGE by 0.47pp.[INDICES] The standout cross-asset move was USO -4.36% as crude sold off hard even while UNG gained 2.47% and TLT added 0.24%.[CROSS-ASSET]  

BREADTH — Breadth printed pct_up 58.2% with adv/dec 121/87, uvol/dvol 1.35, and 20/50/200d above-SMA at 51.0%, 59.6%, and 63.9% respectively.[BREADTH] Mag7 median -0.47% versus ex-Mag7 median +0.42% with narrow_leadership=True and SPY +0.14% trailing the +0.42% universe median, so index strong, internals weak does not apply but mega_cap_tilt=False confirms breadth better than the headline leaders.[BREADTH]  

VOLUME — SPY traded at 0.7x average volume and QQQ at 1.0x sector proxy average via ETFs while IJR ran 1.2x and DIA 1.1x, indicating more attention on small and Dow components than the broad S&P.[VOLUME BREADTH] XLB at 1.8x with price +1.18% looked like accumulation while XLK at 0.8x with price +1.25% signaled a lower-conviction tech ramp and XLE at 0.6x with price +1.15% showed energy strength on thin participation.[VOLUME BREADTH][SECTORS]  

SECTORS — XLU +1.86%, XLK +1.25%, XLB +1.18%, XLE +1.15%, XLI +1.04%, XLRE +0.51%, XLF +0.06%, XLP -0.24%, XLY -0.51%, XLV -0.97%, XLC -1.76% defined the tape, with utilities and tech leading while comms and healthcare lagged.[SECTORS] Industry leaders were SOXX +5.79% and URA +5.70% while laggards were XBI -4.39% and IBIT -6.03%.[INDUSTRIES]  

ROTATION — Money flowed into semis, uranium, and metals via SOXX +5.79%, URA +5.70%, and XME +4.21% while rotating out of cloud, fintech, and biotech with WCLD -3.28%, ARKF -3.51%, and XBI -4.39%.[INDUSTRIES] Defensives_minus_cyclicals at -1.28pp alongside XLK +1.25% and XLE +1.15% versus XLP -0.24% and XLU +1.86% points to a cyclical tilt with AI hardware leading.[BREADTH][SECTORS][FACTOR REGIME]  

THE 7s — Top baskets were Semi +7.71%, Fab +4.87%, New Metals +2.64%, Buildout +2.32%, and Power +2.17%, with MRVL +32.52% the star in Semi and likely NVDA or AVGO leading Fab.[THE 7s][VOL SPIKES][MOVER MRVL][MOVER The Semi 7] Bottom baskets were Software -5.03%, Toll Road -2.28%, New Staples -1.74%, Medtech -1.55%, and Defense -1.17%, with weakest components likely high-duration software in Software and CME -2.80% in Toll Road.[THE 7s][VOL SPIKES]  

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC is June 17, 2026 with CME probabilities at 100% hold, 0% cut, and 0% hike while the stance tally shows 6 hawks, 5 neutral, and 4 doves.[FED POLICY] USD liquidity stress scored 51/100 (Elevated) with the CP−Tbill spread at the 86th percentile as the most-stressed component and reserve scarcity at the 77th percentile, while TED spread at 0.09% and ON-RRP at $1.9B stayed benign.[USD LIQUIDITY STRESS]  

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol at 1.19 and QQQ PCR_vol at 1.35, both with positive skew, indicate solid downside protection demand despite spot strength.[OPTIONS INTEL] VIX at 16.3 below its 20-day average of 17.4 and MOVE at 70.9 below its 20-day 75.0 signal contained equity and bond-vol fear, but IWM PCR_vol at 2.36 and elevated skew highlight aggressive small-cap hedging around the 300.0 gamma flip.[VOL SIGNALS][OPTIONS INTEL]  

CROSS-ASSET — Oil’s USO -4.36% and GLD -1.33% declines alongside VIXY -2.07% and TLT +0.24% show a risk-on equity bid funded partly by unwinds in commodities hedges rather than a pure growth-scare move.[CROSS-ASSET] SLV -3.18% with UUP flat and HYG roughly unchanged at -0.06% points to position-cleanup in metals more than broad credit stress.[CROSS-ASSET]  

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed at 61.6/100 prints clear Greed.[FEAR & GREED] Biggest single movers were MRVL +32.52% in The Semi 7, IBIT -6.03% in crypto-linked Industrials, and XBI -4.39% in biotech, with MRVL’s spike driven by Nvidia’s CEO calling it a “next trillion-dollar company.”[VOL SPIKES][INDUSTRIES][MOVER MRVL]  

WATCH — MRVL +32.52% deserves focus for follow-through or giveback after a 3.6x volume spike on AI-leadership headlines and its key role inside The Semi 7 basket.[VOL SPIKES][MOVER MRVL][THE 7s] XLU +1.86% with weak 20d momentum at -5.33% and elevated sector narrative around power demand makes it a candidate for sustained catch-up, and XLC -1.76% on 1.4x volume may flag further de-rating risk in communication services leaders.[SECTORS][VOLUME BREADTH][SECTOR XLU][SECTOR XLC]
